mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp month-over-month over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal yields a positive 60-day return in >50% of cases over a 5-year rolling sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening economic activity and corporate output, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months where INDPRO increases month-over-month over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 208.0, elevated inflation expectations trigger central bank tightening fears and compress equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 208.0 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 20-day returns in >60% of observed months over a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product signals expanding corporate revenue bases and higher earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 shows positive quarter-over-quarter growth.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent indicates a severe supply-side contraction or demand shock, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >55% of months where INDPRO falls >1.0% month-over-month over a 3-year window.
